paper_title: Bag of Dims: Training-Free Mechanistic Interpretability via Dimension-Level Sign Patterns

claims.claim: Sign encodes content, magnitude encodes confidence in transformer hidden states

evidence.description: Sign-only next-token prediction preserves 60-93% top-5 accuracy through LM head
Pure Hamming scoring (no decoder) reaches 80-90% top-4096 across models
Low-magnitude dimensions act as noise; 800 highest-magnitude dims outperform all dims

claims.claim: Transformer hidden dimensions are independent — pairwise mutual information is near-zero

evidence.description: Mean MI between dimension signs: 0.001-0.005 bits type-level, 0.0004-0.0006 bits contextual
Context lowers rather than creates pairwise coupling
MLP with full cross-dimension capacity adds zero AUC over per-dim logistic regression

claims.claim: Zero-training feature discovery: 175 semantic categories detectable at AUC 0.97-0.99

evidence.description: 161-175/175 categories discoverable (threshold >0.75 per-dim AUC), prototype AUC 0.975-0.993
Trained probe adds only +0.018 AUC and converges to axis-aligned weights (99.9% sign agreement)
Unsupervised discovery: 1500 features (100% yield, 99% sparsity) across all four models

claims.claim: Features are causally operative — flipping sign patterns suppresses concepts

evidence.description: Flipping feature sign pattern suppresses concept: -5 to -24% across models
Effect is magnitude-matched (sign, not magnitude) and concept-specific
Requires coalition-level flipping; individual dimensions are inert

claims.claim: Features survive attention projections and FFN neurons write axis-aligned sign patterns

evidence.description: All 175 categories exceed null calibration in both K and V projections
Up to 20% of features link to individual writer neurons (>0.70 sign agreement)
Top-200 neuron coalitions reconstruct 99.9% of prototypes via majority vote

claims.claim: Cross-modality universality: same structure appears in vision and audio transformers

evidence.description: AST (audio): 50/50 categories, highest per-dim AUC across all models
Pure Hamming 1-NN: 93-97% accuracy across all modalities
